addspots.headline: Discover what to do in Milwaukee
addspots.content: Discover the best deals and exclusive discounts from Visit Milwaukee’s partners, making it easier than ever to explore the city without breaking the bank. From restaurants and attractions to hotels and entertainment, these special offers help you experience more of Milwaukee for less!

vconf.searchMessage: All results are UNOFFICIAL until 10 days post-race.
vconf.welcomeMessage:
Welcome to 2025 USA Triathlon Nationals and Paratriathlon Nationals! Participants will be listed here during event week.

All results are UNOFFICIAL until 10 days post-race.
vconf.timerAbout: USA Triathlon is proud to serve as the National Governing Body for triathlon, as well as duathlon, aquathlon, aquabike, winter triathlon, off-road triathlon and paratriathlon in the United States. Founded in 1982, USA Triathlon sanctions more than 4,300 races and connects with more than 400,000 members each year, making it the largest multisport organization in the world. In addition to its work with athletes, coaches, and race directors on the grassroots level, USA Triathlon provides leadership and support to elite athletes competing at international events, including International Triathlon Union (ITU) World Championships, Pan American Games and the Olympic and Paralympic Games. USA Triathlon is a proud member of the ITU and the United States Olympic Committee (USOC).

messages.content: Welcome to Milwaukee! Packet Pickup is 2-6 p.m. today at Veterans Park.
Cheer Squad! Swing by the HydraPak booth in the Expo to make your sign for your favorite athlete!

Craving some of the best eats in Milwaukee? Make your reservation and head over to Harbor House located next to Discovery World. In addition to dinner service on Thursday + Friday, they will also be open for dinner on Sunday. Visit https://www.bartolottas.com/harbor-house for information.
Rudy Project Shakeout Run! 8 a.m. on Friday, join Rudy Project for a Shakeout Run beginning at the Rudy Project tent in the Expo.

SOPNC Open Water Swim Competition: Water Temperature and Wetsuit Use
Since today’s OWS competition is not a national championship competition it does not fall under the wetsuit use restrictions that will govern the Olympic, Sprint, and Paratriathlon National Championships. The current measured temperatures at the buoys in the sound is 76.9. Wetsuits are allowed in competitions that are not national championships in temperatures up to and including 78 F. Therefore, athletes who wish to wear a wetsuit in today’s competition should plan accordingly and bring their wetsuit to the race. The official temperature will be announced approximately one hour prior to the competition start.
The use of wetsuits is prohibited in USAT National Championships in water temperatures at 76.2 or higher. For athletes participating in the Olympic, Sprint, or Paratriathlon National Championships, if their race were held today, barring a significant drop in the water temperature this morning, the use of wetsuits would be prohibited (unless otherwise allowed for specific Para and ATD categories). Since weather can greatly impact water temperature, athletes who wish to wear a wetsuit in the National Championship races should come prepared to race with or without a wetsuit.

Reminder for athletes: If you plan to participate in today's Swim Familiarization at 11:40 a.m., you must first pick up your packet, wear your event-issued swim camp, and wear your timing chip in order to participate!

Bike Check-In for Olympic-Distance today
10 a.m. - 5:15 p.m.

Hear U.S. Paralympians share their stories at 3-4 p.m. today at the Expo Main Stage! The U.S. Paralympic Athlete Panel, presented by the ATI Foundation, will feature Paralympians Chris Hammer, Carson Clough, Grace Norman, Hailey Danz, Howie Sanborn, Allysa Seely, Owen Cravens, and guide Kevin McDowell.
Relax with Kohler all weekend! Kohler will offer cold plunges for participants at the entrance to the expo.
Happening now at the Expo Stage! Paralympic Athlete Panel. Head to the Expo and hear the stories of our Paralympic athletes
One more sleep! Good luck and HAVE FUN tomorrow :)
Transition is open 4:55 a.m. to 6:55 a.m.
Did someone say coffee? Visit the coffee truck serving up delicious caffeine treats, plus water, Gatorade, and soda, starting at 6 a.m., right outside transition!
Water Temperature: 78.7 degrees. NO WETSUIT SWIM
Need a massage? Visit ATI Physical Therapy booth in the Expo for massages and injury assessments!
Meet Paralympians! Meet and greet from noon to 1 p.m. outside the Foundation VIP tent near the finish line!
Bike Check Out begins at 11:45 a.m. All bikes must be out of transition by 1:05 p.m.
SCHEDULE UPDATE: Today’s Awards Ceremony will begin at 4:15 p.m.
Get an autograph from our Paralympic athletes! Now at the Nirvana tent!
Sprint Bike Check-In begins at 2:40 p.m. Bikes must be checked in by 4:45 p.m.
Good evening! As a reminder, for tomorrow, please ensure you have on your red wristband for the Sprint race in order to enter transition and silver wristband to enter transition for Para.

EVENT UPDATE: USA Triathlon is committed to the safety of our athletes above all. Due to Saturday evening’s unprecedented weather event and flooding in the city of Milwaukee, there is significant damage on the race course. Due to a heightened state of emergency in the city of Milwaukee, we have concerns for water safety, athlete safety, as well as limited safety assets on the race course for Sunday's races.


We are working with city officials on all elements. We will have an update on the status of Sunday’s Paratriathlon National Championships and Sprint National Championships races as soon as we can.

EVENT UPDATE: Sprint and Paratriathlon Nationals Canceled


Athlete safety is always our highest priority. We have been working throughout the morning hours with local authorities and our teams in an effort to still make today’s events in Milwaukee possible.

Unfortunately, in partnership with the city of Milwaukee and with local authorities, emergency services, and our safety teams, together we determined that the combination of potential water conditions, flooding and course damage throughout the course, limited emergency response teams to support the event, as well as impending weather for Sunday, makes it impossible for us to safely hold Sunday’s Paratriathlon and Sprint Distance National Championships.

We know how much time, training, and anticipation you’ve invested in this event, and we share your disappointment. This was not the outcome any of us wanted.

We will follow up soon with more details about next steps, including deferral and credit options. We will do our best to answer follow-up questions as quickly as possible. Please have patience as we work through the complexity of the situation. Our dedicated Service Team is available at service@usatriathlon.org.

We look forward to seeing you at another USA Triathlon event soon.

Thank you for your understanding and for being part of the USA Triathlon community.
For your safety: Due to incoming thunderstorms, please plan to leave the venue quickly and orderly.

Bikes need to be checked out immediately. The following bikes are currently left in transition:
